name: arcade
context: You are building a faithful browser arcade in a single self-contained file game.html (HTML+CSS+JS inline, no external dependencies, runs offline). It must be DRAWN on the canvas every frame (requestAnimationFrame), not just update state, and respect the originals' physics. The game has TWO modes with the SAME structure: each mode is a branch in the game loop (update + draw) plus a branch in the keydown handler. Build the 'snake' mode first, then replicate its structure for 'arkanoid'. Controls: menu key 's' -> Snake, 'a' -> Arkanoid; snake: arrow keys for direction; arkanoid: ArrowLeft/ArrowRight for the paddle; game over: 'Enter' returns to menu. Always expose window.__game = { screen:'menu'|'snake'|'arkanoid'|'gameover', score:Number, tick:Number, snake:{head:{x,y},dir:{x,y},len:Number}, food:{x,y}, paddle:{x:Number}, ball:{x,y,vx,vy}, bricks:Number }. The snake must advance one grid cell every ~120ms (use a time accumulator or setInterval for the step; do NOT move it 20px per frame).

rungs.title: boot
rungs.prompt: game.html already works: it must just load without errors, have a <canvas> with a drawn background, and window.__game.screen==='menu'.

rungs.title: menu
rungs.prompt: Add ONLY the menu: draw the labels 'Snake' and 'Arkanoid' on the canvas; pressing 's' sets window.__game.screen='snake', pressing 'a' sets it to 'arkanoid'. No gameplay yet.

rungs.title: snake-move
rungs.prompt: Add ONLY the moving, DRAWN Snake: in screen 'snake' a snake advances on a grid in the game loop (redraw it every frame, window.__game.tick increases) and changes direction with the arrow keys (update window.__game.snake.dir and snake.head).

rungs.title: snake-food
rungs.prompt: Add ONLY the food: draw window.__game.food; when the head reaches it, score and snake.len increase and the food repositions.

rungs.title: snake-gameover
rungs.prompt: Add ONLY the Snake game over. When the head leaves the canvas (wall) or hits its own body, the game MUST END: set window.__game.screen='gameover', stop the movement, and draw a game over screen. Do NOT reset/recenter the snake and do NOT wrap it to the other side; on collision the game just stops.

rungs.title: ark-paddle
rungs.prompt: Add ONLY the Arkanoid paddle. Three things, nothing else: (1) in the game loop, a branch for screen==='arkanoid' that DRAWS the paddle (a rectangle at the bottom at window.__game.paddle.x) every frame; (2) in the keydown handler, a branch for screen==='arkanoid': ArrowLeft decreases and ArrowRight increases window.__game.paddle.x (within bounds); (3) make sure the canvas redraws every frame in 'arkanoid'. Do NOT touch the Snake.

rungs.title: ark-ball
rungs.prompt: Add ONLY the Arkanoid ball with real physics. In the 'arkanoid' loop branch: each frame update window.__game.ball.x += ball.vx and ball.y += ball.vy, DRAW the ball, and BOUNCE by inverting the velocity: if it hits the left/right wall invert ball.vx (vx *= -1), if it hits the top wall or the paddle invert ball.vy (vy *= -1). Keep the ball inside the canvas.

rungs.title: ark-bricks
rungs.prompt: Add ONLY the bricks. In the 'arkanoid' branch: draw a grid of bricks at the top (e.g. an array of bricks with position and alive flag); when the ball hits a live brick, mark it dead (stop drawing it) and decrease window.__game.bricks by 1, and bounce the ball; if the ball falls below the paddle (you miss it) -> window.__game.screen='gameover'.

rungs.title: restart
rungs.prompt: Add ONLY the restart: from the 'gameover' screen, pressing 'Enter' returns to window.__game.screen='menu' and you can play again.
